channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Fujifilm's epic 40TB tape cartridge is here - despite what Elon Musk says, the future of offline data storage looks very bright
item.description: Fujifilm’s 40TB LTO-10 tape reinforces offline storage relevance as enterprises balance ransomware risk, regulatory demands, and growing AI driven archival volumes.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: The banality of Jeffery Epstein’s expanding online world
item.description: In recent weeks, a project called Jmail.world has quickly recreated the online life of Jeffrey Epstein, the late financier and convicted sex offender with myriad ties to the rich and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: How to watch The Hunting Wives online for FREE from anywhere
item.description: Want to watch The Hunting Wives for FREE from anywhere? Stream every new twist as Sophie is drawn into betrayal, hidden agendas, and a fight to prove her innocence.
item.dc:creator: Krishi Chowdhary, TechRadar

item.title: ServiceNow $12 billion deal spree is ‘deja vu’ of CEO’s SAP days
item.description: After years of eschewing big mergers, ServiceNow Inc. is on a deal spree. It has spent at least $12 billion this year on acquisitions or strategic investments.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: How we can live with AI without losing our humanity
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter covers the latest artificial intelligence technology advancements, including the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoD expands login options beyond CAC
item.description: The memo does not include any requirements for authenticators to be phishing-resistant, even though DoD is required to retire phishable authenticators by 2027. The post DoD expands login options beyond CAC first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: 5 surprising ways to use AI
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. I like pushing AI to be less predictable. When AI assistants are less bland and more bold, they challenge my blind spots and nudge me to rethink.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: The DXC program empowering neurodivergent IT pros
item.description: The tech industry stands to gain significant value by tapping into neurodivergent talent. Such professionals can offer fresh perspectives, demonstrate strengths in areas critical to IT, and even have the potential to make teams 30% more productive, according to research from Deloitte.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to watch Royal Christmas Message 2025
item.description: King Charles III will deliver the traditional festive broadcast for the fourth time. Here's how to watch Royal Christmas Message 2025.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: Time to switch to eSIM? South Korea will now make consumers scan their faces to buy a SIM card and cut down on scams
item.description: South Korea now requires facial scans for SIM registration following data breaches which used fraudulently activated mobile phone numbers.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: The hidden cost of launching AI initiatives without strategic clarity
item.description: In the 1980s and 1990s, many large manufacturing companies pursued an offshoring strategy — not always because a careful analysis showed a clear link between offshoring and achieving their business objectives, but because their competitors… The post The hidden cost of launching AI initiatives withou
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Is the Internet's time wrong? NIST warns servers may have lapsed due to a power outage
item.description: NIST warns specific public time servers may be inaccurate after a power outage disrupted atomic clock distribution.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Google is betting on carbon capture to lower data center emissions. Here’s how the tech works
item.description: As AI data centers spring up across the country, their energy demand and resulting greenhouse gas emissions are raising concerns.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Waymo to update software across fleet after major power failure
item.description: Alphabet Inc.'s Waymo said it would strengthen its automated software to better handle power outages after its self-driving taxis froze and caused traffic jams during a major electricity failure last weekend in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: What Apple has planned for its 50th anniversary year in 2026
item.description: Apple will be 50 years old on April 1, 2026. What can we expect from the company in the year ahead? This is actually the first of two important milestones for Apple; the next anniversary comes in 2027, which will mark 20 years since the introduction of the world-changing iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3D-printed housing project for student apartments takes shape
item.description: Denmark's 3D-printed student village proves automation builds 36 apartments faster than traditional methods. Skovsporet project shows housing future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google says no to training AI on its search results
item.description: Google is suing SerpApi, a web-scraping company that provides its customers with an API that mimics human searching, the latest salvo in the battle over access to data for training and operating AI large language models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
